channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The destruction going on at U.S. government sites is bad news for us all
item.description: Earlier this week, a doctor friend told me about a frustrating new obstacle he’s facing at work. In normal times, he’s relied on websites operated by the U.S. federal government for practical information on everything from vaccine side effects to advice for families traveling to exotic areas.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Why this cybersecurity startup wants to watermark everything
item.description: Cybersecurity startup EchoMark is releasing a new application programming interface (API) to allow for its novel digital watermarking tool to integrate with virtually any existing communications software.
item.dc:creator: Jared Keller, Fast Company

item.title: A Buffy the Vampire Slayer reboot could be headed to Hulu, and I’m both excited and nervous for the return of my favorite TV show
item.description: Hulu has ordered a pilot episode for a Buffy the Vampire Slayer reboot, and although I'm worried that it could flop, I can't wait to see Sarah Michelle Gellar as Buffy Summers again.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Panasonic may leave the TV business, and that would be a crying shame
item.description: Japanese electronics manufacturer Panasonic is considering a sale of its TV business, only a year after it re-entered the US market.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Oppo is launching the world's thinnest foldable in two weeks – and the OnePlus Open 2 should follow soon
item.description: Get ready for the thinnest foldable yet, though we're not yet sure where in the world it's going to be sold.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The next Battlefield game will arrive by April 2026, here’s how you could play it early
item.description: EA confirms a new Battlefield game will be here by April 2026 and is currently looking for playtesters.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Future AirPods Pro Models Could Get Revolutionary Touch Controls
item.description: A new patent by Apple suggests future AirPods Pro models could have more extensive touch controls, allowing for easier volume and playback adjustments even with gloves on.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Adobe's AI assistant can now decipher contract jargon in your PDFs
item.description: Acrobat AI Assistant can now handle contracts to make sure you don’t miss important details obscured in complex language.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Robinhood halts Super Bowl betting contracts after CFTC request
item.description: Robinhood said on Tuesday it is rolling back the event contracts that would let users bet on the result of the Super Bowl clash this weekend, after the online brokerage received a request from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: New US Tariff on Chinese Goods Expected to Impact Prices for American Consumers
item.description: A new 10% tariff on all Chinese goods takes effect, expected to increase prices for consumers on a wide array of products, from cheap apparel to electronics and car parts.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple launches Invites, its event invitation app that takes on Partiful
item.description: Apple rolled out its newest iPhone app called Invites, which lets iCloud+ subscribers create and share invitations to anyone, regardless of whether they have an Apple account or Apple device. Hosts have control over the events.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Goodbye digital transformation, hello AI-first business transformation
item.description: Perhaps we got digital transformation wrong this whole time. Instead of focusing on the transformation part, we did a lot less transforming and a lot more digitalization.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk Faces Off with OpenAI in Court Over For-Profit Conversion
item.description: Elon Musk's lawyers clash with OpenAI in court over the billionaire's request to block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Automated builds, tests, and quality gates are key to software quality – report
item.description: When it comes to software quality, the top three practices cited for production readiness include automated building and deploying, automated tests, and automated quality gates for every merge request, according to the JetBrains Qodana State of Software Quality Report 2024.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: Google’s digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn’t enough to offset investors’ worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be as lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California universities launch “landmark” AI quest for students and profs
item.description: The California State University system has teamed up with several tech titans to launch a "landmark" AI quest.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence
item.description: "I think we think of AI as primarily a bunch of the software geeks who are out there coding somewhere, but there's real hardware involved," said Neil Chilson. The post A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Twitch has plenty of competition in the livestream economy. CEO Dan Clancy isn’t worried
item.description: Business leaders are often reluctant to speak about their competition. It’s rare that you’ll hear Netflix’s Ted Sarandos talk about Disney+, or Skims’s Jens Grede speak about Spanx. It’s uncouth and unhelpful, a good PR will tell you.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Did extreme overclockers inspire Microsoft to develop closed loop liquid cooling for data centers? I'd like to think so
item.description: Microsoft is switching to closed-loop liquid cooling, as used by PC overclockers, for its data centers to eliminate water wastage.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin mimics the moon's gravity for NASA experiments during spaceflight
item.description: Jeff Bezos' rocket company has given NASA a brief taste of the moon's gravity without straying too far from home.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Public cloud providers are missing the mark with AI
item.description: Microsoft’s recent earnings call painted a sobering picture. Despite the company’s aggressive push into artificial intelligence and substantial investments in infrastructure, growth numbers fell short of expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Another Chinese company flexes AI muscle
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
